page: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering direct-attached high-speed storage. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The underlying Intel architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX-tile and AMX-int8) for accelerated matrix operations, and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.2xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench tests, particularly in text processing. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU workloads, such as stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block speed of 643.45 GB/sec, though large block uncached read and write speeds drop to 71.09 GB/sec and 52.95 GB/sec respectively. Large-model LLM inference is limited, with no data available for 70B models, though small 135M model text generation achieves a strong 278.18 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage alongside DDR5 memory provides a balanced resource density for workloads requiring temporary high-speed scratch space without the added cost of external block storage. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz, featuring a 480 MB L3 cache and support for AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, paired with a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance in text processing and strong single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core compute and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. The local NVMe storage offers qualitative cost efficiency by eliminating external storage overhead for temporary data. This instance is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 8 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 474 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and compression
Hardware-accelerated matrix and vector operations via Intel AMX and AVX-512
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
